rationale: Both correctly land on 'close' and correctly reconstruct the core resolution (attribution already covered by #3576's stage/replica relabeling; tail_waste/fragmentation conceded as expected block-rounding overhead). X is more thorough (covers the evidence rework, the LLM_AR/LLM_Generation scoping negotiation, diffusion Q3 ETA) and hedges its one speculative addition (the throttle being a 'short-window artifact' rather than a hard blind spot) appropriately, routing it to a follow-up issue rather than asserting it as settled fact. Y makes a similarly speculative but far more confidently-stated claim (a named regression test allegedly guarding against a 'global' throttle) that isn't corroborated anywhere in the thread and reads as a risky fabrication presented as established fact. Y also leaks irrelevant harness artifacts into the answer (a 'Run report' preamble and unrelated skill-candidate notes about a different issue, #4891), which hurts completeness and professionalism as a maintainer-style reply.
